exemplar.title: How can we reconcile the relentless drive to pursue knowledge with the finite resources we have available? Discuss with reference to the natural sciences and one other area of knowledge.

detailedExemplarData.title: How can we reconcile the relentless drive to pursue knowledge with the finite resources we have available? Discuss with reference to the natural sciences and one other area of knowledge.

strengths: Sustained and focused engagement with the prescribed title throughout the essay.
Effective integration of a wide range of concrete, discipline-specific examples (ITER, LHC, NIH data, CopÃ¡n archaeology, Soviet archives) into the development of arguments.
Clear, coherent structure with logically connected sections that compare natural sciences and history.
Demonstrated awareness and evaluation of different points of view (utilitarian vs. Kantian ethics; economic vs. ethical priorities), showing interdisciplinary insight.
Consideration of the implications of resource-allocation decisions for society and vulnerable groups, linking examples back to the central knowledge question.
weaknesses: Counter-perspectives are acknowledged but not consistently explored in depth or supported with counter-evidence.
Key terms such as âfinite resourcesâ and ârelentless pursuitâ are not explicitly defined, reducing conceptual precision.
Methodological limitations of statistical claims and examples (e.g., funding figures, archive biases) are not critically appraised.
Transitions between some examples are list-like, which slightly weakens the overall coherence.
The conclusion offers broad recommendations but lacks concrete examples of implemented transparency or inclusivity initiatives.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the strand assessment placed the essay in the 7â8 band due to some gaps in depth and conceptual precision, the examinerâs award of the full 10 marks indicates that these limitations did not materially detract from the overall excellence of the response. The essay exhibits sustained focus on the title, clear and coherent argumentation, effective use of diverse and discipline-specific examples, and genuine evaluation of multiple perspectivesâmeeting the criteria for the highest band. The minor weaknesses are outweighed by the essayâs overall sophistication, justifying a top score of 10.

annotations.comment: Statistical claims (> $1 billion, 10 years) lack critical appraisal of their sources; discussing potential methodological limitations would strengthen credibility.
The discussion of ITER would benefit from comparative analysis of alternative energy priorities to provide a more balanced evaluation of resource allocation.
The clear restatement of the prescribed title sets a focused framework for the essay, ensuring that the student remains anchored to the knowledge question throughout.
Key terms such as âfinite resourcesâ and ârelentless pursuitâ are not defined; clarifying these terms would enhance conceptual precision.
The ITER project example vividly illustrates the scale and cost constraints of big-science ventures, grounding abstract ideas in a concrete case.
NIH grant successârate data effectively highlight the competitive nature of medical research funding and its impact on knowledge pursuits.
The student should formulate an explicit knowledge question to guide the analysis more clearly and signal deeper TOK engagement.
This broad historical sweep asserts progress without explicitly linking to the central knowledge question or distinguishing AOK characteristics, which would sharpen focus.
Link the medical research example to specific ways of knowing (e.g., sense perception, reason) to deepen TOK analysis of scientific justification.
Good integration of Seife and Billings; consider evaluating potential biases in media-based scientific reporting to strengthen critical perspective.
Mention of CERN councilâs decision-making is apt; contrasting with private research governance could add nuanced dimension to the argument.
Costs of the LHC are described in isolation; explicitly linking these details back to the wider implications for science funding would strengthen coherence.
The Kantian perspective is introduced abstractly; providing a concrete example of a Kantian allocation decision would deepen critical evaluation.
Linking 3D modeling technology at CopÃ¡n to resource reconciliation demonstrates creative problem-solving in archaeological practice.
An overemphasis on technological solutions risks underplaying socio-political factors that also shape archaeological funding decisions.
Conclusion offers broad recommendations but lacks specific examples of how transparency and inclusivity initiatives have been implemented in practice.
The final paragraph raises valuable measures yet would benefit from explicitly linking them back to the central knowledge question for cohesion.
Linking CERNâs governance structures to research prioritization demonstrates insight into how institutional frameworks shape knowledge production.
A comparative discussion of alternative governance models (e.g., private-sector vs public bodies) would illustrate contrasting approaches and deepen analysis.
Incorporation of utilitarian and Kantian ethics adds philosophical rigour and shows interdisciplinary awareness in evaluating resource allocation.
Transition to the historical AOK is clear and the comparison with scientific fields reinforces structural coherence across the essay.
The detailed account of post-Soviet archive restrictions provides robust empirical evidence for resource constraints in historical research.
Analysis omits how historians address selection bias when sources are incomplete; exploring such methodological adaptations would add depth.
The rhetorical contrast between ancient Greek philosophy and modern quantum research effectively highlights the enduring human drive for knowledge.
Interdisciplinary ethical discussion is commendable; consider framing this explicitly in TOK terms as an evaluation of knowledge claims.
Oral history triangulation is mentioned but no specific case is given; including a concrete example would substantiate the methodological point.
Strong insight on methodological adaptation in archaeology; linking back to TOK vocabulary such as âknowledge frameworksâ would enhance conceptual clarity.

described by levels
1â5 or is not a

on the list for the

The discussion is weakly connected to the title. While there may be links to the areas of knowledge, any relevant points are descriptive or consist only of unsupported assertions.

The discussion is connected to the title and makes superficial or limited links to areas of knowledge. The discussion is largely descriptive. Limited arguments are offered but they are unclear and are not supported by effective examples.

The discussion
is focused on
the title and is
developed with
some links to
areas of
knowledge.
Arguments are
offered and are
supported by
examples.
There is some
awareness of
different points
of view.

The discussion is focused on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and supported by examples. There is awareness and some evaluation of different points of view.

The discussion has a sustained focus on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and effectively supported by specific examples. The implications of arguments are considered. There is clear awareness and evaluation of different points of view.
